description: A tortilla folded or layered around melted cheese and chicken, then cooked on a griddle or pan until crisp outside and hot inside. It is often served with salsa, sour cream, or guacamole.
preparation_steps: Prepare the chicken and any vegetables for the filling.
Place cheese and chicken on one half of a tortilla or between two tortillas.
Cook on a pan or griddle until the tortilla is crisp and the cheese melts.
Slice and serve with dips or condiments.

health_advice.benefits: Provides protein and can be filling, especially when served with salsa or vegetables.
health_advice.risks: Can be high in calories, sodium, and saturated fat if loaded with cheese and fried heavily.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use moderate cheese, lean chicken, and add vegetables for better balance.

dietary_notes.note: Chicken quesadillas are non-vegetarian by default, but vegetarian and vegan versions are common. Gluten-free versions require gluten-free tortillas.

allergens: Gluten in flour tortillas unless gluten-free tortillas are used
